pro: holds a strong prior, grounded in their reading of the literature, that the exposure has a real and beneficial (positive) effect on the outcome
anti: holds a strong prior, grounded in their reading of the literature, that the exposure has a real and harmful (negative) effect on the outcome
neutral: holds no strong prior about the direction or existence of the effect and aims to let the data speak for itself
believer: is convinced, based on their reading of the literature, that the effect is real and substantial -- a strong, non-zero relationship between exposure and outcome
skeptic: is convinced, based on their reading of the literature, that the effect is negligible or non-existent once the data are analyzed carefully
